title: What is the Navigator.share() Web Share API?
description: How do you share content natively from the browser?
options: navigator.share({title, text, url}) invokes the native OS share dialog; navigator.canShare() checks if sharing specific data is supported. Requires HTTPS and user gesture. Can share files with navigator.share({files})
correct_answer: navigator.share({title, text, url}) invokes the native OS share dialog; navigator.canShare() checks if sharing specific data is supported. Requires HTTPS and user gesture. Can share files with navigator.share({files})

title: Implement a JavaScript object pool pattern.
description: Create an object pool for efficient reuse.

title: What is the difference between Error and AggregateError?
options: AggregateError wraps multiple errors into one; thrown by Promise.any() when all promises reject. Access individual errors via .errors array. Useful for operations that can fail in multiple ways simultaneously
correct_answer: AggregateError wraps multiple errors into one; thrown by Promise.any() when all promises reject. Access individual errors via .errors array. Useful for operations that can fail in multiple ways simultaneously

title: Implement a JavaScript immutable update helper.
description: Create utility functions for immutable state updates.

title: What is the Scheduler.postTask API?
description: How does postTask work for task scheduling?
options: scheduler.postTask(callback, {priority}) schedules tasks with priority levels: 'user-blocking' (urgent), 'user-visible' (default), 'background' (low). Provides browser-native task prioritization, replacing manual requestIdleCallback/setTimeout patterns
correct_answer: scheduler.postTask(callback, {priority}) schedules tasks with priority levels: 'user-blocking' (urgent), 'user-visible' (default), 'background' (low). Provides browser-native task prioritization, replacing manual requestIdleCallback/setTimeout patterns

title: Implement a JavaScript stream processor.
description: Create a transform stream that processes data chunks.

title: What is the difference between requestAnimationFrame and requestIdleCallback?
description: When to use rAF vs rIC?
options: requestAnimationFrame runs before next paint (~16ms for 60fps), ideal for animations/visual updates. requestIdleCallback runs during browser idle time, ideal for non-urgent work (analytics, prefetching). rAF is high priority, rIC is low priority
They run at the same time
correct_answer: requestAnimationFrame runs before next paint (~16ms for 60fps), ideal for animations/visual updates. requestIdleCallback runs during browser idle time, ideal for non-urgent work (analytics, prefetching). rAF is high priority, rIC is low priority

title: Implement a JavaScript Circuit Breaker pattern.
description: Create a circuit breaker for resilient API calls.

title: What is the Compression Streams API?
description: How do you compress/decompress data in the browser?
options: CompressionStream and DecompressionStream provide gzip/deflate compression in the browser; used with ReadableStream.pipeThrough(). new CompressionStream('gzip') creates a compressor. Useful for reducing upload sizes and processing compressed responses
correct_answer: CompressionStream and DecompressionStream provide gzip/deflate compression in the browser; used with ReadableStream.pipeThrough(). new CompressionStream('gzip') creates a compressor. Useful for reducing upload sizes and processing compressed responses

title: Implement a JavaScript async generator for pagination.
